content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 3, 1991

Beatrice A. Bermudez, Treasurer
Citizens for Education
14109 Foster Road
La Mirada, CA 90638

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File Nos. I-91-379 &
I-91-387

Dear Ms. Bermudez:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of Citizens for Education and the Bermudez for
School Board Committee concerning the campaign disclosure and
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ Subsequent telephone conversations with you and other
members of the two organizations have provided additional
information which has been incorporated in our discussion.
Because your request is general in nature and we do not have
sufficient facts concerning a specific circumstance, we are able
to provide only the following informal assistance² and a general
overview of the Act's requirements.

Your letter of August 8, 1991, states that Citizens for
Education ("Citizens") was formed in 1991 to "ensure that
Proposition 98 would not be suspended or manipulated." Citizens
filed a Statement of Organization (Form 410) as a recipient
committee. Rudy Bermudez is the chair for Citizens, and also has

_______________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Beatrice A. Bermudez
I-91-379 & I-91-387
Page 2


filed appropriate documents to run for the school board in the
Norwalk-La Mirada Unified School District. Mr. Bermudez has
established the Bermudez for School Board committee (the "Bermudez
Committee").

Since receiving your letter, Wayne Imberi of the Commission
staff has had at least three telephone conversations with you and
other members of the two organizations. First, Mr. Imberi
explained that Citizens for Education does not appear to be a
"committee" pursuant to the Act. Section 82013 of the Act states
that a "committee" includes a person or group of persons that
receives "contributions" totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar
year. Under the Act and Commission regulations, the term
"contribution" does not include donations to organizations formed
to monitor initiative measures that have already been passed into
law.

Mr. Imberi also explained that once Mr. Bermudez became a
candidate, his position as chair of Citizens could qualify it as a
"controlled" committee if Citizens makes contributions to his
school board campaign or sends mailings at his behest. In
addition, the Act does not allow Mr. Bermudez to control both the
Bermudez Committee and Citizens if Citizens becomes a committee.

According to Mr. Imberi, you indicated that Citizens would
file a Statement of Termination and would not make any
expenditures in connection with Mr. Bermudez' candidacy.

On Friday, September 13, 1991, Mr. Imberi received another
telephone call from a member of the organization stating that Rudy
Bermudez would resign as chair of Citizens and that you and Mr.
Bermudez' father would take his place. This factor alone will not
change Citizens' current status nor keep it from qualifying as a
controlled committee. Using Citizens' funds to make contributions
to the Bermudez Committee or to send mailings at Mr. Bermudez'
behest may still create filing obligations for Citizens and you
should contact this office again if you plan to make such
expenditures.

Your letter to Susan Bobrow dated August 7, 1991, asks if Mr.
Bermudez, as chair of Citizens, would have a conflict of interest
if he distributed a Citizens flyer concerning state budget funding
for education and its effect on the Norwalk-La Mirada Unified
School District. Such activities may be considered contributions
to Mr. Bermudez, whether he chairs Citizens at the time the flyers
are distributed or not. However, such activities do not come
under the Act's conflict of interest provisions, which prohibit a
public official from making or participating in making
governmental decisions in which the official has a financial
interest.

Beatrice A. Bermudez
I-91-379 & I-91-387
Page 3


     If you need further information, please do not hesitate to
contact Mr. Imberi again at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                   By:   Carla Wardlow
                                        Assistant Division Chief
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                             Analysis Division
